_note: Clay's overemployment scoring defaults v3.0 per 2026-04-24 directive. PER-ROLE pay range $44,444-$133,333 (min/max relative to any one specific job). NO CUMULATIVE STACK MAX — higher total is better; aspirational, unbounded. N stack: 3 minimum / 5 target / 7 with hedged backups (vs v2.1's fixed 7); risk tolerance tightened to 0.8333 (matching pacing-calibration thresh, allowing Detection Risk 2 jobs); pay preference HOURLY over annual.
_stack_goal_note: No cumulative cap. Higher stack total is better; aspirational/unbounded. Per Clay correction 2026-04-24: 'no min and max was relative to any one specific job, there is no cumulative max (higher is better)'.
_pay_bands_note: v3.0 extended to absorb hourly preference + ceiling at 3x target. Annual-equivalent bands shown; hourly pay_position scoring uses annual_equivalent = hourly * 2080 working hours (40 hrs * 52 weeks).
_dimensions_v3_note: v3.0 expands from v2.1's 8 dimensions to 13+ per Clay's 'aiming for 13+ dimensions' directive. New dimensions: Pay Type Preference (hourly favored), Backup Hedging Suitability (employer fragility/replaceability), Onboarding Compressibility (can role be ramped fast?), Geographic Anonymity (W-2 disclosure surface), Classification Flexibility (1099 vs W-2 negotiability per role).

_classification_caveats.federal_government_public: STRICT LEGAL implications for holding multiple federal/government/public roles concurrently. Pivoting INTO federal = drop-everything-and-pivot career changeover; cannot hold concurrent stack. Defense companies + 3p contractors (cleared but private-sector) are viable workaround. Establishing sole-employee corporate entity for consulting is alternative path.
_classification_caveats.1099_negotiation: Per Clay 2026-04-24: open question whether companies routinely allow conversion from W-2 offer to 1099 contractor classification. If permissible, allows quantifying benefits as direct pay (overemployment-optimized employment). Legal + tax implications differ; requires per-employer + per-state review.
_classification_caveats._1099_negotiation_factor_note: Per Clay 2026-04-24 directive: target 1.6666x the W-2 base rate when negotiating 1099 conversion. More aggressive than industry-standard 1.3-1.5x. Justification: contractor absorbs self-employment tax (15.3% combined) + benefits cost + admin overhead; the standard 1.3-1.5x covers benefits-equivalent only. 1.6666x covers benefits + tax burden + admin + market premium. Pattern-matches Clay's 0.8333 risk tolerance + 83.33% pacing-calibration threshold (1/0.6 = 1.6666; 1 - 1/6 = 0.8333).
_classification_caveats.concurrent_legality: Beyond federal restrictions: most employer non-compete + moonlighting clauses are state-specific. CA/OR/CO have strong employee-favorable rules; TX/FL more employer-favorable. Per-jurisdiction review required.

changes: stack_goal $311,108 -> null (NO CUMULATIVE MAX; higher is better; aspirational)
per_job_ceiling new: $133,333 (PER-ROLE max; relative to any one specific job)
pay_max removed; replaced with pay_max_per_role $133,333 + pay_max_cumulative null
added: pay_type_hourly_preference, backup_hedging_suitability, onboarding_compressibility, geographic_anonymity, classification_flexibility, concurrent_role_compatibility
added: classification_caveats block (federal/government restrictions, 1099 negotiation, concurrent legality)
Correction per Clay: stack_goal was incorrectly set to 133332 (3x target); corrected to null. The $44,444-$133,333 range is PER-ROLE only; no cumulative cap exists.
